---
title: "Data Science"
---


<div style="display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; justify-content: center; gap: 20px;">
  <a href="http://go.osu.edu/datasciencetraining"><img src="/img/stemcoding_osu_DS_training_2026.png" width=385 ></a>
  <a href="https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSfGzzp_CyNeiOwBH-f_WMKvz9Wy3QvO2xjy13a9Heh2kh1oGg/viewform"><img src="/img/Texas_flyer_2026.png" width=385 ></a>
</div>


Nationally there is a movement in high schools to offer "data science" as an alternative to algebra 2 level math, and to enhance traditional science courses with data-related tasks and spreadsheet skills. This is music to our ears in the STEMcoding project where we work to re-imagine introductory science and math courses!

<!--
<img src="/img/stemcoding_data_science_icon_2026.png" width=426 style="display: block; margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;" >
-->
The first STEMcoding data science activity was released back in 2017 with what would eventually become our <a href="http://go.osu.edu/earthdaycoding">Earth Day Hour of Code</a> activity. We've been building data science content ever since with the help of [multiple](https://u.osu.edu/stemcoding/2021/10/29/stemcoding-data-science-selected-for-2021-aip-meggers-award/) [grants](https://u.osu.edu/stemcoding/2025/07/20/stemcoding-wins-20k-grant-for-data-science/) for curriculum development.

Our high school data science curriculum emphasizes spreadsheet skills with Google Sheets and Excel. Importantly, our activities align with the [intro level Excel industry credential](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/certifications/mos-excel-2019/#certification-exams) so that students in a year long data science course with our curriculum will be ready to take the certification exam (which is not as crushingly difficult as other industry credential exams). Now we are ready to share [our curriculum](activities.html#category=Data\ Science) with the world!

<!--
For two weeks starting July 7, 2026 we will offer online and hybrid (in person + online) training to teach this curriculum.
-->

<a href="https://reg-continuinged.osu.edu/search/publicCourseSearchDetails.do?method=load&amp;courseId=1585149&amp;sectionId=1585469&amp;showInternal=true#courseSectionDetails_1736619"><img style="margin-top: 0; margin-bottom: 0; position: relative; left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%);" src="/img/stemcoding_continuing_ed.png" alt="" width="600" /></a>

Our new [continuing education course](https://reg-continuinged.osu.edu/search/publicCourseSearchDetails.do?method=load&amp;courseId=1585149&amp;sectionId=1585469&amp;showInternal=true#courseSectionDetails_1736619) is open to teachers everywhere. The online only option will meet daily via Zoom while the hybrid option will meet in person at OSU Marion for July 7 &amp; 8 and online for July 9 - 17 (except for the weekend). The cost of the course includes software for you to use in your classroom. There is an early bird discount, so sign up soon!  Upon completing the course, participants will receive a certificate stating they have completed up to 3.0 Continuing Education Units (CEUs).



<b>Texas teachers</b> can get our online training for free in late June through our partnership with [WeTeach_CS](https://weteachcs.org/training-events/preferred-curriculum-and-pd-provider-workshops/2026/) at the University of Texas at Austin. Fill out this [interest survey](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSfGzzp_CyNeiOwBH-f_WMKvz9Wy3QvO2xjy13a9Heh2kh1oGg/viewform) or reach out to [stemcodingohio@gmail.com](mailto:stemcodingohio@gmail.com) if you have questions

<!--
<a href="http://bit.ly/STEMcodingDS26"><img src="/img/Texas_flyer_2026.png" width=650 style="display: block; margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;" ></a>
-->
